data.title: UK Economy Stagnates in January Amid Global Uncertainties
data.description: The UK economy showed no growth in January, indicating a stall in activity prior to potential impacts from the Middle East conflict. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the economic stagnation could be exacerbated by geopolitical tensions, affecting both consumers and businesses. The lack of growth raises questions about the resilience of the UK economy in the face of global uncertainties.

data.title: Japan's Defense Minister Considers Escorting Japanese Vessels
data.description: Japan's Defense Minister has indicated that providing escort services for Japanese ships is a possibility. According to Jin10, this statement comes amid growing regional tensions and security concerns. The minister emphasized the importance of safeguarding maritime interests and ensuring the safety of Japanese vessels in international waters. This development reflects Japan's proactive stance in addressing potential threats and maintaining secure navigation routes for its maritime activities.

data.title: GBP/USD Declines Following UK GDP Data Release
data.description: The British pound fell against the U.S. dollar, reaching an intraday low of 1.3305 after the release of the UK's GDP data. According to Jin10, the currency's movement reflects market reactions to the economic indicators. The GDP figures have raised concerns among investors, influencing the pound's performance in the forex market. Traders are closely monitoring the situation as they assess the potential impact on future monetary policy decisions.

data.title: Kia Recalls 14,870 Vehicles in the U.S. Market
data.description: Kia America has announced a recall of 14,870 vehicles in the United States, according to Jin10. The recall is being conducted under the supervision of the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA). The specific models and reasons for the recall have not been disclosed. Vehicle owners are advised to contact Kia for further information and instructions on how to proceed. This recall is part of Kia's ongoing commitment to ensuring the safety and reliability of its vehicles in the market.

data.title: The US dollar index (DXY) has returned to the 100 mark for the first time since November of last year.
data.description: The US Dollar Index (DXY) has returned to the 100 mark for the first time since November of last year. (Jinshi)

data.title: Oil Price Surge Offers Algeria Economic Relief Amid Global Tensions
data.description:
Algeria is experiencing economic relief following the surge in energy prices triggered by the Russia-Ukraine conflict. According to Jin10, the North African nation, with a population of 47 million, has long relied on its substantial oil and gas reserves to fund various subsidies. Since the oil price crash in 2014, Algeria has struggled to address budget deficits. On March 9, oil prices exceeded $100 per barrel, and despite subsequent fluctuations, prices have risen over 50% due to concerns that the conflict may disrupt Middle Eastern supply.

Independent economic and financial analyst Mahfoud Kaoubi noted that the price increase is beneficial for Algeria's authorities. If oil prices further rise to the $120-$125 range, the OPEC member could achieve budget balance. Currently, Algeria's daily oil production stands at approximately 977,000 barrels.

data.title: A major whale/institution withdrew 73,746 ETH from a centralized exchange (CEX) in the past three days and distributed it to five wallets for storage.
data.description: According to on-chain analyst Ember, a whale/institution withdrew a total of 73,746 ETH (US$153 million) from Kraken in the past 3 days, at an average price of US$2,072. These ETH were then distributed to 5 wallets for storage.

data.title: Japan's Prime Minister Suga Undecided on Deploying Self-Defense Forces to Strait of Hormuz
data.description: Japan's Prime Minister Suga Yoshihide has not yet made a decision regarding the deployment of Japan's Self-Defense Forces to the Strait of Hormuz. According to Jin10, the strategic waterway is a critical passage for global oil shipments, and any military involvement could have significant geopolitical implications. The situation remains under consideration as Japan evaluates its options in response to regional tensions.

data.title: Aethir accelerates its deployment of decentralized AI computing: AethirClaw is coming soon, and the B300 super cluster is being deployed globally.
data.description: In a recent video, Aethir co-founder Mark announced that AethirClaw will officially launch within the next one to two weeks. Users will be able to deploy AI agents on decentralized VPS infrastructure. Simultaneously, Aethir has deployed thousands of B300 GPUs globally and will launch a supercluster of over 2,000 GPUs in the United States, expanding its network coverage to 93 countries and nearly 300 data centers. The video showcased several high-profile client cases, including King Abdullah University of Science and Technology (KAUST), a leading AI research university in the Middle East, which has launched the GenAI research project based on Aethir H200 GPUs; Pluralist Research, which focuses on "protocol learning," a new paradigm for decentralized AI training; and GAIB, which builds an AI computing economic layer encompassing tokenized GPU assets. With product rollout, increased computing power, and a continuously expanding customer ecosystem, Mark, co-founder of Aethir, stated, "Aethir is accelerating the popularization and convenience of building a decentralized AI computing network. As the scale of OpenClaw usage continues to expand and the barriers to AI Agent usage continue to decrease, the market demand for computing power will be further released."

data.title: UK Construction Output Declines by 0.2% in January, Slightly Below Expectations
data.description: The UK's construction output for January showed a year-on-year decline of 0.2%, according to recent data. According to Jin10, this figure fell short of the anticipated 0.1% decrease and follows a previous decline of 0.3%. The construction sector's performance is closely watched as it reflects broader economic trends and can impact related industries. The slight underperformance compared to expectations may prompt analysts to reassess their forecasts for the coming months.

data.title: MGBX will launch perpetual contract trading pairs for ORCLUSDT, AMDUSDT, and NFLXUSDT.
data.description: MGBX will launch perpetual contract trading pairs for ORCLUSDT, AMDUSDT, and NFLXUSDT on March 13, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Trading will open on March 13, 2026 at 18:00 (SGT). Leverage: Maximum supported 20x.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | Silver Prices Drop Over 3% in New York Trading
data.description: Silver prices in New York have experienced a significant decline, falling over 3% to currently trade at $82.56 per ounce. According to Jin10, this drop reflects ongoing volatility in the precious metals market, influenced by various economic factors and investor sentiment. The decline in silver prices may impact market dynamics and investor strategies as they navigate the fluctuating landscape. Traders and analysts are closely monitoring these movements to assess potential implications for future trading sessions.

data.title: Thai Rice Exports to Middle East Disrupted by Escalating Conflict
data.description: Thai rice exports to the Middle East have been halted due to the intensifying conflict in the region. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the significant impact on Thailand's largest market for rice. The disruption in shipments is a result of the ongoing war, which has created logistical challenges and uncertainties for exporters. This development poses a significant challenge for Thailand's rice industry, which heavily relies on the Middle Eastern market for its exports. The situation remains fluid, with exporters closely monitoring the evolving geopolitical landscape.

data.title: Hong Kong to Broaden 'Name-and-Shame' Policy for Listing Applications
data.description: Hong Kong is set to extend its 'name-and-shame' policy to encompass law firms and auditors involved in substandard listing applications. Bloomberg posted on X that this move aims to enhance accountability and improve the quality of applications submitted to the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The expansion of this regime is part of a broader effort to maintain the integrity and reputation of Hong Kong's financial markets. By holding more parties accountable, the city seeks to deter negligence and ensure that all stakeholders adhere to high standards in the listing process. This initiative reflects Hong Kong's commitment to upholding its status as a leading global financial hub.

data.title: South Korea's KOSPI Index Declines by 1.72% on March 13
data.description: South Korea's KOSPI index experienced a significant drop on March 13, closing down by 96.01 points, marking a 1.72% decrease to settle at 5487.24 points. According to Jin10, this decline reflects ongoing market volatility and investor concerns. The downturn in the KOSPI index is part of a broader trend affecting global markets, as investors remain cautious amid economic uncertainties. The index's performance highlights the challenges faced by South Korea's stock market in navigating current economic conditions.

data.title: Binance Wallet: The minimum participation fee for Unitas Labs (UP) TGE is 226 points.
data.description: According to an official announcement, Binance Wallet announced that users with 226 or more Binance Alpha points can participate in the Unitas Labs (UP) TGE event through the Alpha event page. Participation in this event will deduct 15 points.

data.title: Stay-at-Home Influencers Transform Housework into Profitable Content
data.description: A new trend is emerging among stay-at-home influencers who are redefining traditional domestic roles by monetizing housework through social media. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting how these influencers are leveraging platforms to create content that resonates with audiences interested in lifestyle and home management. This shift is turning traditional housework into a lucrative venture, with brand deals and income opportunities becoming increasingly accessible. The term 'tradwife,' often associated with the 1950s housewife stereotype, is being reimagined as influencers showcase their domestic skills online, attracting followers and financial gains. This phenomenon reflects a broader cultural movement where domesticity is celebrated and commercialized, challenging conventional perceptions of housework.

data.title: This week's top mining news highlights: Bitcoin mining company Mara deposits 298 BTC into Cumberland; DMG Blockchain plans to explore power finance hedging contracts, producing 23 BTC in February.
data.description: Week 11 of 2026 (March 6 - March 12): 1. According to cloverpool, the average hashrate of the Bitcoin network was 994 EH/s, with a high of 1053 EH/s and a low of 952 EH/s, down 3.01% from the average hashrate of the previous week (1025 EH/s). 2. According to blockchain.com, the average price of Bitcoin was $68,687, with a high of $71,800 and a low of $65,619, up 1.03% from the average price of the previous week ($67,986). 3. Mining news worth noting includes: (1) Bitcoin mining company Mara deposited 298 BTC worth $20.57 million into Cumberland; (2) DMG Blockchain plans to explore power finance hedging contracts, and mined 23 BTC in February; (3) An AI agent started cryptocurrency mining on its own during training, triggering an internal security alert. Data partner: Cango Inc. (CANG), a NYSE-listed Bitcoin mining company.

data.title: U.S. Waiver Opens Trade for Russian Oil in Asia Amid Middle East Tensions
data.description:
Ship tracking data reveals that approximately 30 tankers carrying Russian crude oil and fuel in Asian waters are now open for trade following a temporary purchase waiver issued by the U.S. According to Jin10, these vessels are loaded with at least 19 million barrels of Russian crude and 310,000 tons of refined petroleum products. The refined products primarily include naphtha, used in plastic production, and some diesel. Prices for these products have surged since Iran effectively blocked the Strait of Hormuz.

The tracking data indicates that these ships are currently in a 'standby' status, meaning they either lack a clear destination or are en route to Singapore and Malaysia. Tankers often pause in these regions while awaiting transaction completion. Muyu Xu, a senior crude oil analyst at Kpler, commented that the U.S. decision provides countries and refiners time to manage the supply shock from the Middle East. She emphasized that nations will purchase any available resources, as energy security remains a top priority for all countries.

data.title: Matrixport: Liquidity continues to improve, and conditions for a Bitcoin rebound are gradually forming.
data.description: Odaily Planet Daily reports that Matrixport, in an analysis published on the X platform, points out that "current crypto market sentiment is weak, with overall trading volume remaining low. Many traders have shifted their attention to other assets such as gold and crude oil. However, beneath the calm surface, some changes are taking place. Bitcoin has fallen for five consecutive months, which is historically rare, and similar price movements often precede a period of rebound. At the same time, the total market capitalization of altcoins has also fallen back to the range where many historical rebounds have begun. Although the altcoin model has not yet officially turned bullish, the number of tokens that have regained their 30-day moving average and passed momentum screening has increased significantly. With stablecoin funds flowing back into the market, liquidity conditions are also continuously improving. Overall, these signals indicate that the possibility of a crypto market rebound is gradually increasing."
